title: Shannon (1948): Boolean Algebra + Probability -> Information Theory
bridge_from: S = -k sum p_i ln p_i (Boltzmann entropy)
breakthrough: A precise, mathematical theory of communication. Information is measurable. Founded all of modern communications, data compression, cryptography, and ML.

title: Hopfield (1982): Statistical Physics + Neuroscience -> Neural Networks
bridge_to: E = -1/2 sum w_ij s_i s_j (Hopfield energy)
breakthrough: Memories as energy minima. Retrieval = gradient descent. Directly inspired Boltzmann Machines and deep learning.

title: Black-Scholes (1973): Brownian Motion + Economics -> Options Pricing
bridge_to: dV/dt + 1/2 sigma2 S2 d2V/dS2 + rS dV/dS - rV = 0
breakthrough: Option prices derived from no-arbitrage + stochastic calculus. Created the modern derivatives market (~$600 trillion notional).

title: Turing (1952): Reaction-Diffusion + Biology -> Morphogenesis
breakthrough: Biological patterns from homogeneous initial state + noise. Explained leopard spots, fish stripes, fingerprint ridges.

title: Jaynes (1957): Statistical Mechanics + Bayesian Inference -> MaxEnt
breakthrough: Statistical mechanics is not just physics - it is a general inference algorithm. Unified thermodynamics, Bayesian statistics, and information theory.

title: Hodgkin-Huxley (1952): Electrical Circuits + Neuroscience
bridge_to: C_m dV/dt = -g_Na m3 h(V-E_Na) - g_K n4(V-E_K) - g_L(V-E_L) + I_ext
breakthrough: Quantitative prediction of action potentials from first principles. Founded computational neuroscience. Nobel Prize 1963.
